transcript: Ever had a moment where everything just clicks like an idea so obvious you can't believe you didn't see it sooner I've been chasing ideas trying to find the right one but then I saw this and hit me this isn't just about food. It's about everything. You know how when you finish a meal, and you're totally full. Like you can't even fathom being hungry ever again. Like your body is full of food, and you're sitting there thinking I don't ever remember what hunger feels like. I can't even look at food right now. The same thing happens in life. Once you start accomplishing the things that actually matter to you, the things that make you feel happy, you get the same sense of fullness. Full on the feeling of, wow, my time is finally going where I want it to go. The things on your list are actually getting done. The promises you make to yourself, you're finally keeping them. And as you get full on those little wins, something interesting happens. Oh, sorry, continue. You start to forget what it's like to be stuck. To be that lazy procrastinating version of yourself who always made promises but never followed through. That version of you it fades and suddenly you're the person who shows up, who finishes things and moves forward. So here's what I want you to do. Look at your list. What's at the top? What's that one thing you keep putting off? Make the first step happen. Just one step. Commit to it. Finish it. And see how it feels. You don't need to fix everything today. Just pick one thing. See when you start, you'll feel it, that first wind, that first sense of fullness. And soon, when you look back at the version of yourself who was stuck, you'll barely Thank you for watching. You'll feel it, that first win, that first sense of fullness. And soon, when you look back at the version of yourself who was stuck, you'll barely recognize them. You'll think, how did I ever live that way? And the best part, you'll feel so full. We as humans, we adapt to our current feelings like that. To the good and the bad, it's why we get stuck, and it's also why we can get unstuck. All it takes is one choice, one step, one promise kept, and then, you'll wonder why you didn't start sooner.
